title.zh: 媽咪學歷飆升，兒女升學關鍵悄然轉向：母職變奏中的教育新局
title.en: Mothers’ Rising Status: The Hidden Engine of College Completion

summary.zh:
儘管現代社會中母親的社會地位大幅提升，但過去研究多認為母親對子女大學畢業的影響與父親相近。然而這項研究揭開新發現：雖然母親地位提升的「回報率」（每單位地位對子女大學畢業的影響）與父親類似，但因母親的教育程度、職業地位和收入普遍提高，這使得近年出生的世代中，母親的地位對子女大學畢業的貢獻比例，已超越父親。研究透過三項大型美國全國性調查數據，證實了這一現象——母親的教育水平、職業階級與收入的躍升，正悄然重塑子女升學的關鍵動能。

更令人驚訝的是，研究的分解分析顯示，過去數十年間女性教育程度的快速成長，使得家庭生活水準大幅提升，而這直接反映在子女大學畢業率的上升。尤其對於1960年代後出生的世代，母親地位的提升對大學畢業率的貢獻，比父親更為顯著。這項發現也解釋為何過去研究多忽略母親角色的關鍵變化：當母親成為家庭經濟支柱，其社會地位的提升便成為教育普及的隱形推手。
summary.en:
Despite decades of progress in women’s social and economic standing, the role of mothers in shaping children’s educational trajectories has remained understudied. A recent analysis of U.S. national survey data reveals that while the returns to mothers’ status (measured by education, occupation, and earnings) have remained stable and comparable to fathers’ across cohorts born from the 1930s to the 1980s, the sheer increase in mothers’ status levels has driven greater contributions to rising college completion rates—particularly for children born after 1960. This shift, overlooked in prior research, underscores how women’s advancement has become a critical but hidden engine of educational progress.

The study’s decomposition analysis shows that the rapid rise in women’s educational attainment and economic power has amplified the effect of mothers’ status on college completion. As single-parent families have become more common and mothers increasingly serve as primary breadwinners, their elevated status has become a more significant factor in children’s academic success. This nuanced finding challenges the assumption that parental influence on education is gender-neutral and highlights the need for future research to account for the distinct pathways through which mothers and fathers shape their children’s futures.

keyArticle.title: The Changing Role of Mothers’ Status in Children’s College Completion
